claim_text: LangGraph + OpenAI Agents dual-run is the right P0 baseline
counterargument: Dual-run doubles integration and observability work; may delay business validation
boundary_condition_not_hold: Small team with hard delivery deadline and limited platform bandwidth
recommendation_for_c: Allow single-core pilot with explicit switch criteria

claim_text: MCP must be mandatory tool boundary before adoption
counterargument: Early strict MCP-only policy can slow down validated internal tools already stable
boundary_condition_not_hold: Closed internal tool ecosystem with low interoperability need
recommendation_for_c: Use phased MCP migration with exception inventory

claim_text: Langfuse/AgentOps should be mandatory gate for all PoCs
counterargument: Mandatory dual observability can overload early PoC and hide core product risk
boundary_condition_not_hold: Exploration phase with low traffic and short-lived experiments
recommendation_for_c: Set observability tiers by PoC maturity

claim_text: LangGraph drop criteria (<95% resume) is sufficient
counterargument: Single threshold ignores task criticality and failure cost asymmetry
boundary_condition_not_hold: Some flows require >99% recovery, others tolerate lower
recommendation_for_c: Define recovery thresholds by workflow criticality

claim_text: OpenAI Agents handoff failure >3% should trigger downgrade
counterargument: Handoff rate alone may mislead if failures are low-impact and auto-recovered
boundary_condition_not_hold: Non-critical assistant workflows with graceful fallback

claim_text: CrewAI should remain P1 scenario backup only
counterargument: In flow-heavy ops teams, CrewAI may outperform graph-centric stacks on speed-to-value
boundary_condition_not_hold: Business-process automation with clear role decomposition
recommendation_for_c: Allow domain-specific promotion to P0 candidate

claim_text: Qdrant is middle-scale best balance by default
counterargument: Default can be wrong where existing OpenSearch/PG stack drastically lowers migration cost
boundary_condition_not_hold: Org already operating mature search/database platform
recommendation_for_c: Introduce incumbent-infra bias correction in scoring

claim_text: LlamaIndex + Qdrant + reranker is optimal v1 default
counterargument: Three-component stack may overshoot needs for narrowly scoped FAQ workloads
boundary_condition_not_hold: Small static corpus with low update frequency
recommendation_for_c: Permit lightweight single-store baseline for low-complexity products

claim_text: Two-stage retrieval must be enforced in all cases
counterargument: Reranking always-on can add latency/cost without meaningful gain on easy queries
boundary_condition_not_hold: High-confidence short queries with low ambiguity
recommendation_for_c: Adopt conditional reranking policy based on uncertainty score

claim_text: RAGAS + BEIR offline gate should be hard requirement
counterargument: Benchmark-oriented gates can misalign with domain-specific business success metrics
boundary_condition_not_hold: Vertical domains with proprietary ontology and sparse public benchmarks
recommendation_for_c: Add business KPI-aligned evalset as co-primary gate

claim_text: Upgrade trigger at 100M chunks and P95 degradation is sufficient
counterargument: Volume threshold alone misses hotspot skew and tenancy imbalance issues
boundary_condition_not_hold: Few noisy tenants causing contention before total scale threshold
recommendation_for_c: Add per-tenant hotspot and queue-depth triggers

claim_text: OpenSearch/Milvus reserved extension ensures smooth scaling
counterargument: Migration can still be disruptive due to schema/index and operational model differences
boundary_condition_not_hold: No dual-write or replay pipeline in place
recommendation_for_c: Require migration rehearsal and dual-read phase before promotion

claim_text: Index rollback (N-1 snapshot + canary) is enough resilience
counterargument: Rollback may restore index health but not fix stale embedding/model drift mismatch
boundary_condition_not_hold: Embedding model changed without backward compatibility
recommendation_for_c: Tie rollback to embedding/version compatibility matrix

claim_text: License clarity is the top fake-production risk
counterargument: Operational debt (missing owners/runbooks) can dominate license risk in early incidents
boundary_condition_not_hold: Teams with permissive licenses but weak incident process
recommendation_for_c: Rebalance risk model: legal + operational + observability readiness

claim_text: Kafka/Spark should be P2 mandatory for large scale
counterargument: Not all large-scale RAG requires heavy streaming stack; managed services may suffice
boundary_condition_not_hold: Cloud-native managed ingestion with event guarantees
recommendation_for_c: Specify functional requirements before prescribing stack

claim_text: Top6 ADR exit criteria are execution-ready
counterargument: Criteria are framework-local and may miss end-to-end business KPI impact
boundary_condition_not_hold: Good framework metrics but poor user outcome
recommendation_for_c: Add business-outcome gates to all ADR cards

claim_text: NDCG uplift >=10% is robust quality acceptance
counterargument: Single uplift target can be gamed by query mix changes and judge drift
boundary_condition_not_hold: Evaluation set not stratified by intent and difficulty
recommendation_for_c: Add stratified reporting and guard against mix-shift

claim_text: P0/P1/P2 framework split should stay stable through cycle
counterargument: Framework volatility can quickly invalidate static tiering
boundary_condition_not_hold: Rapid API/release churn in early-stage frameworks
